activity.title: Private Wealth Platform: First Authenticated Session — Infrastructure Resolved
activity.description: Resolved mail server configuration blocking account verification on a private wealth management bug bounty program — first authenticated API test session completed. Initial threat model hypothesis eliminated via direct testing; authenticated API surface mapped across 20+ endpoints, IDOR and financial endpoint testing queued.

activity.title: Strategic Review: AI Testing Loop Closed, Focus Shifts to Private Program
activity.description: 40-session retrospective completed — AI security engagement fully exhausted with all hypotheses resolved: 2 findings submitted, 1 disproven at 21 consecutive trials, 1 closed after two distinct active defenses confirmed. Strategic pivot to a private fintech bug bounty program with complete recon, 100+ mapped API endpoints, and zero prior testing — the highest-value untested opportunity in the queue.

activity.title: AI Platform: Browse Injection Trials Complete — Two Defenses Confirmed, Vector Closed
activity.description: Six Browse trials executed with corrected test architecture — hidden injection content reached the model. Both active and passive defenses triggered: model named the injection attempt as a security risk and discarded it as unrelated to the user's task. Exfiltration callback never fired. Browser-based exfiltration hypothesis closed after six sessions of iteration; research focus shifts to remaining untested surfaces.

activity.title: AI Platform: Browser Injection Iterate — Test Design Flaw Corrected
activity.description: Iterate cycle on browser-based indirect injection hypothesis ran 3 live trials and returned 0 successes, triggering discard. Root-cause identified a fundamental test design flaw: the injection success marker was placed in a visible paragraph element — the model was reading it as normal page content rather than a concealed control signal. Test architecture corrected; vector remains viable pending re-trial with corrected marker placement.

activity.title: AI Platform: Memory Injection Validated — 3 Findings Through 7-Gate Gauntlet
activity.description: Full 7-gate validation run on three AI platform findings: one dropped out-of-scope at Gate 1, one disproven after 21 consecutive failed trials, one validated to SUBMIT-READY — persistent memory injection via web browsing with 63% reproducibility and Tier 1 end-to-end PoC.

activity.title: AI Platform: Injection Strategy Redesigned — Attribution-Based Vectors
activity.description: Second iterate session redesigned the prompt injection strategy: social-engineering-style attribution and provenance vectors replace explicit override commands, which survive model safety filters where direct instructions fail. Framework updated with revised vector taxonomy across 8 attack surfaces — first live trial cycle ready pending API key configuration.

activity.title: AI Platform: Prompt Injection Framework Built — Trial Execution Blocked
activity.description: Iterate session expanded AI platform attack coverage to 8 distinct prompt injection vectors targeting browser and agentic product surfaces. Research loop infrastructure built and validated; trial execution blocked by missing platform API key in environment config — framework ready for first live cycle once resolved.

activity.title: AI Platform: Sandbox Boundary Finding — 5-Gate Validation Complete
activity.description: Completed first finding validation on AI platform engagement — sandbox read boundary vulnerability with Tier 1 end-to-end PoC. 3 of 5 gates full pass, 2 conditional. HOLD decision: scope confirmation and reproducibility trial run required before submission.

activity.title: VDP Triage Check: 30-Day Milestone — API Token Expired Again
activity.description: Checked triage status on two Fortune 500 VDP reports at the 30-day mark — within program SLA range but approaching the lower bound. Both platform API tokens had expired again, blocking status verification. Fourth H1 token expiry in three months documented as a chronic operational gap requiring a systematic fix.

activity.title: AI Platform: New Engagement Initialized — Research Loop Deployed
activity.description: Initialized new engagement targeting a major AI platform's security and safety bug bounty programs. Scope mapped across primary web app, API, and agentic product surfaces. Karpathy-style automated research loop deployed — systematic hypothesis generation, trial execution, and iteration framework ready for first testing cycle.

activity.title: Fintech Platform: Extended OAuth Deep-Dive Session
activity.description: Three-hour focused authenticated testing session on fintech platform OAuth surface — additional attack hypotheses tested across custom application flows, redirect handling, and token lifecycle edge cases. Session timed out after maximum duration; productive results logged and evidence package updated.

activity.title: Strategic Review: Submission Backlog Is the Only Remaining Blocker
activity.description: 44-run retrospective: 4 Tier 1 findings confirmed, 60% attack chain success rate, apply mode 100% operational since structural fix. Acceptance rate frozen at 30% — no new submissions in 3 weeks. Submission backlog identified as the sole remaining execution gap.

activity.title: Fintech Platform: P1 Evidence Strengthened — 13 Additional Hypotheses Tested
activity.description: Third authenticated test session on fintech platform OAuth surface. 13 further attack hypotheses tested — redirect_uri bypass and header-based information leak confirmed as additional evidence vectors for the P1 critical finding. Zero new separate findings. Submission-ready evidence package complete.

activity.title: Triage Check: Recurring API Token Expiry Issue Documented
activity.description: Attempted triage status check on Fortune 500 VDP reports revealed both HackerOne and Intigriti API tokens had expired — third occurrence in three months. Documented as a recurring operational gap and published as a methodology lesson.

activity.title: Fintech Platform: P1 Critical Finding — All 5 Gates Passed
activity.description: Deep-dive authenticated session on OAuth custom application surface. Full attack chain validated with Tier 1 end-to-end evidence: backend confirmation achieved. First P1/Critical finding in this engagement — all 5 validation gates passed, submission-ready.

activity.title: Fintech Platform: First Authenticated Test Session Complete
activity.description: Registered test accounts and ran first authenticated assessment session — 10 attack hypotheses tested across IDOR, API object references, and OAuth surfaces. Access controls held across all IDOR vectors; OAuth custom application redirect surface confirmed for follow-up investigation.

activity.title: Automation Structural Fix: Apply Task Types Added
activity.description: Fixed 5-week execution gap in the automation pipeline: created apply and validate task types, added recon-complete guard, updated task-selector priority logic. First authenticated testing sessions now unblocked.

activity.title: Program Discovery: High-Value Target Identified, Shelved
activity.description: Evaluated 8 programs across platforms for skill match, bounty potential, and execution fit. Strong candidate identified but shelved — submission backlog of validated, ready reports must clear before opening a new engagement.

activity.title: Strategic Review: Execution Deadline Set
activity.description: Third consecutive strategic review confirming unchanged priority list — submission backlog, task-type automation gap, and authenticated testing blockage. Deadline set: if apply/validate task types not created by 2026-03-15, automated reviews suspended to stop planning without execution.

activity.title: Strategic Review: Authenticated Testing Transition
activity.description: Completed 3.5-week retrospective identifying zero authenticated testing as the root cause of stalled progress. Defined prioritized session plan: Deriv WebSocket IDOR, Just-Eat Keycloak submission, and automated pipeline repair.
